g.text: 0x00-0x01 | uint16 | opcode // GET=0x01, SET=0x02, DEL=0x03, INCR=0x04
0x02-0x05 | uint32 | key_len // Length of key in bytes
0x06-0x09 | uint32 | val_len // Length of value (0 for GET/DEL)
0x0A-0x11 | uint64 | ttl_ms // TTL in milliseconds (0 = no expiry)
0x12-N | bytes | key // UTF-8 encoded key string
N+1-M | bytes | value // Raw value bytes
0x0002 | SET | Store key-value with optional TTL

p.strong: All multi-byte integers: Network byte order (Big-Endian)

div.p: Example: key_len = 0x0000000F (15 bytes) Wire format: [0x00][0x00][0x00][0x0F] ↑ most significant byte transmitted first

ul.li: Consider padding to 24 bytes for SIMD operations
Key/value pointers can reference original buffer (zero-copy) Optimization tip: Use encoding/binary.BigEndian for portable parsing, or unsafe casts on little-endian systems with byte swapping.
